_comment: Generated by ontology/export_web_graph.py - do not edit by hand. Rebuild after a pipeline run: python ontology/export_web_graph.py

nodes.label: Incident
nodes.description: One canonical crash incident (one row of the treated table after Same-Incident dedupe).

nodes.label: Vehicle
nodes.description: A vehicle involved in the incident. The subject (ADS/ADAS) vehicle is seeded from columns; crash-partner vehicles come from CP columns or the narrative.

nodes.label: Company
nodes.description: Operating / reporting entity, canonicalized to master_entity.

nodes.label: Location
nodes.description: Where the incident occurred: place plus roadway context.

nodes.label: EnvironmentalCondition
nodes.description: A weather / lighting / roadway condition present at the incident (one node per condition value, shared across incidents).

nodes.label: Automateddrivingsystem
nodes.description: Autonomous Driving System or automation level engaged during the incident (discovered in 46 narratives; e.g. "Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode")

nodes.label: Collisiontype
nodes.description: The type or nature of collision impact (discovered in 8 narratives; e.g. "reversed into the left rear bumper")

nodes.label: Crosswalk
nodes.description: Designated pedestrian crossing areas on roadways (discovered in 9 narratives; e.g. "pedestrian crosswalk")

nodes.label: Damage
nodes.description: Physical damage to a vehicle resulting from an incident (discovered in 24 narratives; e.g. "damage to the rear window")

nodes.label: Direction
nodes.description: The heading or compass direction of vehicle travel (discovered in 17 narratives; e.g. "traveling northbound")

nodes.label: Impactlocation
nodes.description: The specific part or region of a vehicle where physical contact occurred (discovered in 9 narratives; e.g. "front of the passenger car made contact with the rear of the stationary Waymo AV")

nodes.label: Injuryseverity
nodes.description: The medical outcome or injury status of a person involved in the incident (discovered in 8 narratives; e.g. "transported to the hospital")

nodes.label: Lane
nodes.description: A specific lane or traffic lane on a roadway, identified by position and direction (discovered in 55 narratives; e.g. "rightmost dedicated left turn lane on into lane three of three lanes in the northbound direction")

nodes.label: Laneposition
nodes.description: A specific position or location within a traffic lane (discovered in 18 narratives; e.g. "third from the right lane")

nodes.label: Maneuver
nodes.description: An action or movement performed by a vehicle (discovered in 37 narratives; e.g. "began to change lanes to the right")

nodes.label: Operator
nodes.description: A person operating a vehicle, including test drivers and autonomous system operators (discovered in 11 narratives; e.g. "a test driver was operating the Waymo AV in manual mode")

nodes.label: Passenger
nodes.description: A person traveling in a vehicle (discovered in 25 narratives; e.g. "a passenger in the Waymo AV opened the rear left door")

nodes.label: Pedestrian
nodes.description: A person traveling on foot (discovered in 39 narratives; e.g. "a pedestrian that had begun crossing the roadway from the east side")

nodes.label: Person
nodes.description: Individuals involved in or affected by incidents (discovered in 24 narratives; e.g. "individual involved was transported from the scene to a hospital")

nodes.label: Regulatoryreport
nodes.description: Official regulatory reporting requirement or framework (discovered in 17 narratives; e.g. "Standing General Order 2021-01")

nodes.label: Regulatoryrequirement
nodes.description: A rule or order requiring incident reporting or data submission (discovered in 19 narratives; e.g. "Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01")

nodes.label: Roadfeature
nodes.description: Physical structures in the environment that vehicles interact with (e.g., gates, tracks) (discovered in 18 narratives; e.g. "elevated gate track")

nodes.label: Safetyfeature
nodes.description: Equipment or systems designed to enhance safety (discovered in 12 narratives; e.g. "hazard lights")

nodes.label: Testdriver
nodes.description: Human operator present in an autonomous vehicle (discovered in 11 narratives; e.g. "test driver was present (in the driver's seating position)")

nodes.label: Trafficcontrol
nodes.description: Traffic signals and control mechanisms that regulate vehicle movement (discovered in 93 narratives; e.g. "red light")

nodes.label: Vehiclecontact
nodes.description: The specific point or area where two vehicles make physical contact during a collision (discovered in 10 narratives; e.g. "passenger side of the passenger car made contact with the rear driver side of the Waymo AV")

nodes.label: Vehicleoccupant
nodes.description: A person inside or operating a vehicle (discovered in 12 narratives; e.g. "Not explicitly mentioned in narrative")

nodes.label: Vehicleposition
nodes.description: The state of motion or positioning of a vehicle (discovered in 9 narratives; e.g. "came to a stop in a queue of traffic")

nodes.label: Vehiclestate
nodes.description: The operational or physical state of a vehicle at a point in time (discovered in 22 narratives; e.g. "came to a stop in a queue of traffic")

competency_questions: Which companies (operating entities) were involved in the most incidents?
Which companies' vehicles were involved in pedestrian-related incidents, and how many each?
What types of crash-partner vehicle (e.g. SUV, pickup truck, heavy truck) most often collided with the subject autonomous vehicle?
What pre-crash maneuvers most often preceded a collision?
Which crash-partner pre-crash maneuvers most often precede a contact with a stopped or stationary autonomous vehicle?
Which traffic controls (e.g. red light, stop sign, green light) were most frequently present at incidents?
How many incidents occurred while the vehicle's Level 4 ADS was engaged in autonomous mode?
Which environmental conditions co-occur with the highest injury severities?
In which cities and states do incidents occur most frequently?
Which roadway types (street, intersection, parking lot, highway) account for the most incidents?
What is the distribution of highest injury severity across incidents?
Which companies had incidents involving injuries requiring hospitalization?
How often was a test driver or safety operator present in the vehicle, and in which operating mode?
Which vehicle impact locations (e.g. rear, front, side) were most frequently the point of contact in a collision?
Which pre-crash maneuvers most often precede a collision that involves a pedestrian?
How many incidents involved the autonomous vehicle being stationary or stopped when it was struck by another vehicle?
Which incidents were reported under Standing General Order 2021-01 reporting requirements, and by which companies?
In what share of collisions did both the autonomous vehicle and the crash partner sustain damage?
